task: Simplify the trajectory dataset schema so agents can realistically emit training-data rows without excessive burden.
summary: Reworked the canonical trajectory schema from a comprehensive audit-style object into a lean training/eval row with required task, context, trajectory, final fix, outcome, and export gate fields.
observations: docs/trajectory-dataset-schema.md now defines a small DebuggingTrajectoryV1 row whose required fields match the training unit: problem, context, trajectory, final fix, verification, and outcome.

Detailed consent, license, provenance, reproduction setup, knowledge feedback, and pattern taxonomy fields were moved out of the required row into source events, curation systems, or optional curation metadata.
Product docs and agent instruction surfaces now describe Datalox Trajectory Data as lean, outcome-labeled rows rather than compliance-heavy verified objects.
TODO.md validation tasks now focus on required training fields and the small export gate instead of forcing consent/data-rights and reproducibility fields into every row.
